terms.definition: A normalized scalar (0–1) measuring weighted alignment among a civilization’s key subsystems such as family, trust networks, and shared meaning.
The empirically derived χ value (≈0.35) below which positive social feedback loops invert and accelerate systemic fragmentation.
A discontinuous change in macroscopic system behaviour triggered by a small parameter shift, here applied to societal coherence.
A causal cycle in which system outputs feed back into inputs, reinforcing or stabilizing change.
Any technological development that multiplies the velocity of coherence loss by amplifying fragmentation drivers.
A self-reinforcing downward spiral of coherence once the critical threshold is crossed.
